Hey guys, welcome back. As promised, we're just going to look at a little example breakdown from the end justice for all. So this is at the end of your workbook, which you should also see next to this video. So this is also sent in the 10X freelance copywriter launch. I think this was side by side with the Simon Cowell qualifier. So it begins. A modest 45K per year, nights, weekends and holidays, superglue to a Starbucks table. The one next to a wall plug because your 2014 MacBook Air has a lifespan of a diabetic fruit fly. True story. Constantly keeping an eye out for local job openings, because even though you hate to admit it, at this point, you might just be willing to trade in your precious freedom for a fixed salary, 401K and company sponsored trip to the dentist. All your friends seem to love it. Well, love is a strong word. They tolerate it. Okay, they hate their jobs, but they get every weekend off. That means they're happy 28.57% of the time. So this is it. Or is it the life of the average freelance copywriter? So this is really painting that existential moment of highest tension and very specific action. You know, this is something that could be visualized. A night or a weekend at Starbucks, trying to find a plug for their 2014 MacBook Air. The most frustrating part is you have the talent. You continue to sharpen it through books, courses and training. You've seen what average looks like and you're light years ahead of it. So this is really just a very quick dose of that exclusive empowerment based off of some accomplishments, actions or aspirations they have in a perfect world. That should keyword should be enough to grow your freelance income to say double what that stingy hiring manager is trying to strangle you into since he knows he has all the leverage. Only problem is you keep making these same old mistakes and those mistakes can be stuck in freelancing purgatory. So let me help you start by understanding this. Being a freelance copywriter is a super valuable six figure a year gig. It's in its DNA. But why isn't your freelance biz where it should be? So this is where we diagnose that injustice. I might have the answer you seek. I've seen hundreds of freelance copywriters stunt their growth with cringe worthy moves like sending their proposals via email. And the rare case they actually land a call with the nail list client. They handle said call with the same composer as a lifelong trekking next in line to meet Shatner at Comic Con. Stop it please. So here's where we're giving them the spoon. The call to action is helping them break out of that injustice. Little meme action. And then we sell the event. Finally sell the event or the product or whatever the call to action is with some fascinations. But that really only comes at the end once we've created that that injustice loop that has to be closed. We've shown them where they are as an injustice. We've told them where they should be. And we've really created a lot of friction between those two places and a lot of existential urgency to close it. So that is the example. That is the injustice for all super powerful template. Totally encourage you to give that a shot and I'll see you in the next video.
